element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational mechanics for establishing first routes and generating revenue
Higher capacity and efficiency than road, requires more capital investment and planning
Initial rail technology with period-appropriate limitations
Requires canal construction and terraforming; highest capacity but slowest
Fastest transport but expensive to establish and operate
Improved rail technology replacing steam engines
Advanced rail technology with superior characteristics
Requires separate track system and significant capital; futuristic alternative to conventional rail

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-late game (ongoing after introduction of new models)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Experimental designs with improved stats but reliability risks; optional high-risk investment
Environmental manipulation to optimize routes and overcome terrain obstacles
Random events that disrupt operations; difficulty modifier affects frequency

typical_relief_triggers: Profitable year with positive cash flow
typical_tension_triggers: Initial capital loan and interest accrual
Station ratings declining due to inefficiency
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle tension with gradual economic pressure. Each in-game year (~20 minutes real-time) creates recurring tension-relief cycles as players manage cash flow, competition, and aging vehicle fleets. 100-year spanning gameplay (1930-2030) creates macro-level tension arcs around technological transitions.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Industry and town placement on randomly generated maps; player discovers income opportunities through exploration and observation
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Efficient routing optimization; creative use of multi-modal transport chains (truck→ship→train sequences) rewards spatial problem-solving

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low complexity; establish basic road/rail routes, manage initial loan, learn interface mechanics, build first profitable routes
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity; balance multiple competing transport networks, manage vehicle fleet aging, adapt to technology availability, face increasing competitor pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: High complexity; optimize large interconnected networks, evaluate monorail investment, manage mature vehicle fleets with reliability issues, compete for limited profitable routes against established rivals
notable_spikes: Introduction of new vehicle models requiring old fleet replacement decisions
Monorail availability (1999) introducing separate track system complexity

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Random map generation with variable difficulty
notable_level_patterns.description: Every map procedurally generated with configurable terrain, town/industry density, and disaster frequency creating unique economic landscapes each playthrough
Vehicle and infrastructure options unlock chronologically (1930-2030 span), creating natural progression gates and forcing adaptive strategy as outdated tech becomes unavailable
Players combine different transport types into single routes (truck→ship→train), rewarding spatial planning and creative problem-solving for efficiency gains
Interest payments on initial loan create baseline cash pressure; revenue generation and management determine survival and growth trajectory
